source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AVOCA

Avoca is transforming how home service companies engage with their customers. Our AI-powered conversational agents handle every high-value inbound call, from booking jobs, qualifying leads, and driving higher revenue, all at a speed and consistency unmatched by human call centers.

We're a Series B startup backed by Kleiner Perkins, Meritech Capital, General Catalyst, and other top-tier investors, with $125M raised and a $1B valuation. We grew 10x in 2025 and have scaled to 130+ employees in under two years across our NYC headquarters and Santa Barbara office.

In a $1T+ market where 85% of missed calls go to competitors, we're building the category-defining platform for AI-driven customer engagement, serving the largest brands in home services. With high-energy, in-office teams in both locations, every hire here has an immediate and visible impact.







ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Sales Development Representative (SDR) at Avoca, you’ll fuel our growth engine by identifying, engaging, and qualifying prospective customers across the home services industry. You’ll run high-velocity outbound programs, convert interest into meetings for our Account Executives. You'll help evangelize Avoca’s impact by turning missed calls into booked jobs and measurable revenue.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on speed, experimentation, and impact. You’ll own pipeline generation end-to-end: target the right segments, craft compelling outreach, book high-quality meetings, and collaborate closely with Sales, Marketing, and Product to turn customer pain into Avoca wins.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Prospect and qualify leads across home services segments, serving as the first point of contact and trusted resource for prospects evaluating Avoca.

 - Run high-volume, multi-channel outbound (LinkedIn, email, calls, events) to build pipeline and consistently exceed meeting and SQL targets.

 - Tailor outreach to customer goals and business models; articulate Avoca’s value in booking jobs, capturing missed demand, and lifting revenue.

 - Partner with Account Executives to schedule demos, share context, and ensure smooth handoffs that convert into opportunities and closed-won deals.

 - Travel to industry conferences and events to generate leads, book demos on-site, and represent Avoca with professionalism and urgency.

 - Conduct discovery to qualify fit, pain, and timing; map stakeholders across Operations, Marketing, and Executive teams.

 - Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, and Product to refine messaging, share market feedback, and continuously improve playbooks and cadences.

 - Track activity and outcomes with precision; measure and report on KPIs to guide prioritization and iterate quickly.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 1-3 years in an SDR, BDR, or outbound sales role, ideally at a high-growth startup.

 - A track record of hitting or exceeding outbound meeting targets, with meetings that actually convert downstream.

 - Confidence on the phone and in writing: you can articulate value clearly whether it's a 30-second cold call or a personalized email.

 - Comfort with high volume; you can run 50+ touches a day without sacrificing quality.

 - CRM discipline: clean data, updated stages, notes that your AEs can actually use.

 - Nice to have: experience selling AI, SaaS, or into the home services vertical.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You're competitive with yourself. You track your numbers, know your conversion rates, and are always looking for the next lever to pull.

 - You handle rejection well, not because you ignore it, but because you treat it as signal. A "no" teaches you something about messaging, timing, or targeting.

 - You prepare before you pick up the phone. You've looked at the prospect's website, understand their business model, and can speak to their specific pain.

 - You're a clear communicator who keeps AEs, managers, and cross-functional partners in the loop without being asked.

You’ll work directly with founders, operators, and revenue leaders to understand their call flows, conversion challenges, and growth goals, and help them adopt Avoca as a mission-critical revenue engine.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in founder-led sales environments, is comfortable selling a technical product, and wants to help define how Avoca sells as we scale.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the end-to-end sales cycle for mid-market and enterprise home services customers.

 - Run discovery calls to deeply understand customer operations, call workflows, and revenue pain points.

 - Deliver tailored demos and solution pitches that map Avoca’s AI to customer outcomes.

 - Manage pipeline, forecasts, and deal progression with high accuracy.

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to close complex deals.

 - Provide feedback from the field to help shape product roadmap and positioning.

 - Hit and exceed monthly and quarterly revenue targets.

 


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 2–5+ years of closing experience in B2B SaaS (AI, vertical SaaS, or operational software preferred.)

 - Proven ability to run complex sales cycles and close high-ACV deals.

 - Strong discovery skills, you ask great questions and listen closely.

 - Comfort selling to operators, founders, and revenue leaders.

 - Clear, confident communicator who can explain technical concepts simply.

 - Highly organized, proactive, and comfortable in fast-moving environments.

 - Excited to work in-office with a high-ownership team in NYC.

 


WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience selling to home services, SMBs, or operations-heavy businesses.

 - Familiarity with call centers, customer support, or revenue operations.

 - Experience working in early-stage startups.

 - In-office, engaged, and eager to collaborate at our NYC location full-time.

ABOUT THE ROLE

As our GTM Recruiter, you'll partner with the Head of GTM Recruiting to build out Avoca's commercial organization at one of the most pivotal moments in our growth. You'll own end-to-end recruiting across Sales, Customer Success, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, Marketing, and adjacent commercial roles, with the autonomy to shape how we attract, evaluate, and close talent as we scale.

This is a builder's seat. You'll inherit a foundation but have real ownership over candidate experience, sourcing strategy, and the systems that will define how Avoca hires for years to come. You'll work directly with sales and commercial leadership as a trusted partner, and report to our Head of GTM Recruiting.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Run full-cycle recruiting across all GTM functions, holding a high bar on quality and speed while managing multiple searches in parallel.

 - Identify and engage passive talent that isn't on the market yet, building relationships over weeks and months so that when the right role opens, the right person is already in conversation.

 - Shape a candidate experience that becomes a competitive advantage, where every touchpoint reflects our culture, declined candidates refer their network, and our process itself is a reason people say yes.

 - Craft authentic outreach and positioning that cuts through the noise of generic pitches and gets elite candidates to take the call.

 - Partner with hiring leaders on intake, calibration, and closing strategy, influencing decisions with data and conviction.

 - Build the operational backbone, from pipeline tracking, scorecards, sourcing playbooks, that lets us scale without sacrificing quality.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5-7 years in GTM recruiting at high-growth startups (agency backgrounds welcome), ideally early-stage experience.

 - A track record of sourcing and closing top commercial talent, not just running process on inbound.

 - Demonstrated ability to identify passive candidates and build long-arc relationships that convert.

 - Strong stakeholder management: you can push back on a hiring manager and earn their trust at the same time.

 - Comfort managing multiple reqs simultaneously with rigorous follow-through and organization.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication.

 - Ideally fluency in our core stack: Ashby, Noon AI, LinkedIn Recruiter, and Notion.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Builder mentality: you see gaps in process and want to fix them, not flag them.

 - Energized by inflection points and the ambiguity that comes with scaling fast.

 - Thoughtful about candidate experience as a craft, not a checkbox.

 - Self-directed and entrepreneurial, with strong instincts for prioritization.

 - Collaborative and organized, with a high standard for your own work.

We're hiring a Business Operations Manager to work directly with our leadership team. This is a generalist operator role: you'll work across whatever the company needs most, with a meaningful focus on new vertical expansion.

At any given time, that might look like sizing a new market, running a design partner pilot with a PE-backed operator, building a financial model for a leadership decision, or owning a cross-functional initiative that doesn't have a clear home yet. The scope is wide by design. We're at a stage where the right person can shape what this function looks like and what they grow into.

Your work will span two primary areas, with room to flex as the company's priorities evolve.



New Vertical Expansion

 - Structure and run design partner pilots from kickoff to first contracted ARR

 - Lead discovery and implementation calls, engage executive stakeholders and PE-backed operators directly

 - Translate field insights into structured product briefs and work closely with Engineering to scope and prioritize

 - Build repeatable GTM playbooks as verticals graduate from pilot to scale

 - Travel to customer sites and industry events as needed, roughly once a month
   
   

Business Operations and Strategic Projects

Beyond new verticals, you'll work on high-priority initiatives across the company. The specific projects will vary, but the through-line is consistent: own it end-to-end and move fast.

 - Build financial models, business cases, and analysis to support leadership decisions

 - Identify and close gaps in how the company operates, from process, tooling, data, or coordination

 - Support the founders and leadership team on special projects and strategic priorities

 - Act as connective tissue between product, GTM, and customer-facing teams

 - 3-6 years in consulting, investing, or high-growth startups, ideally with some time in an operating role

 - Demonstrated ability to own a complex, cross-functional initiative from start to finish

 - Strong analytical foundation: comfortable building models, sizing markets, and pressure-testing assumptions

 - Comfort operating in ambiguous environments where the problem isn't handed to you

 - Strong written and verbal communication with both operators in the field and executives in the boardroom

 - High agency: you create clarity where none exists and move without waiting to be told

Bonus points:

 - Experience with vertical SaaS, AI-enabled products, or services businesses

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AVOCA

Avoca is transforming how home service companies engage with their customers. Our AI-powered conversational agents handle every high-value inbound call, from booking jobs, qualifying leads, and driving higher revenue, all at a speed and consistency unmatched by human call centers.

In a $1T+ market where 85% of missed calls go to competitors, we're building the category-defining platform for AI-driven customer engagement, serving the largest brands in home services. With high-energy, in-office teams in both locations, every hire here has an immediate and visible impact.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring our first IT Manager to own the full technology environment at Avoca. This is a foundational role sitting within Operations — you'll be responsible for everything from day-to-day employee support and SaaS administration to leading the IT build-out of our NYC headquarters. You'll report to the Head of Operations and work closely with every team in the company as we scale from 100 to 200+ employees.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own IT onboarding for every new hire — configure devices, provision accounts, and ensure day-one readiness across the company

 - Administer Avoca's SaaS stack (Google Workspace, Slack, Notion, HubSpot, Zoom, and others) — managing access, integrations, and licensing

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for all hardware and software issues; keep the team unblocked and moving fast

 - Own endpoint security, identity and access management, and IT policy enforcement across the organization

 - Drive IT process improvement, develop and manage ticketing and escalation workflows, and prepare and refine standard operating procedures

 - Evaluate vendors, run cost-benefit analyses on tool upgrades and renewals, and ensure Avoca is getting best-in-class value

 - Track and manage hardware and software inventory; manage laptop lifecycle from procurement through offboarding

 - Comfortable working with/managing outsourced IT support/MSPs.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of experience in IT management or systems administration, ideally at a high-growth startup

 - Hands-on experience with Google Workspace and a modern MDM platform (Rippling, Jamf, Kandji, or equivalent)

 - Strong networking fundamentals — VLANs, Wi-Fi infrastructure, firewalls, DNS/DHCP

 - Experience with identity and access management tools (Okta, Active Directory, or equivalent)

 - Track record of supporting an office build-out or major infrastructure project end-to-end

 - Strong vendor management and analytical skills; comfortable building a cost-benefit case and making a recommendation

 - Clear, confident communicator who can translate technical decisions for non-technical stakeholders

 - Nice to have: familiarity with AI tooling governance, SaaS security reviews, or relevant certifications (CompTIA, Google IT, AWS)




WHO YOU ARE

 - You're hands-on and ownership-oriented — when something breaks or doesn't exist yet, you fix it or build it without being asked

 - You operate well in ambiguity and move fast; you don't wait for a perfect brief to get started

 - You think in systems — you spot the upstream cause, not just the immediate symptom, and you build for scale even when you're solving for today

 - You take the work seriously and hold a high bar for reliability; the team depends on you and you don't let them down

 - You're in the office five days a week and set the standard for what a well-run, well-supported physical workspace looks like




BENEFITS

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - Company-sponsored 401(k) through Vestwell

 - Flexible PTO plus U.S. federal holidays

 - Company-wide off-sites

 - Daily in-office

In a $1T+ market where 85% of missed calls go to competitors, we're building the category-defining platform for AI-driven customer engagement, serving the largest brands in home services. With high-energy, in-office teams in both locations, every hire here has an immediate and visible impact.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Avoca's SDR org has grown to 14 people (11 SDRs and 3 team leads). We're adding a second SDR Manager to take direct ownership of half that team: a team lead and a pod of reps, running day-to-day coaching, pipeline quality, and ramp. This is a new role reporting directly to Craig (SDR Manager). You're inheriting a proven motion, qualification criteria, CRM workflows, call intelligence tooling, and will be judged from day one on your pod's SQO volume, SQO quality, and rep development.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own a pod of SDRs and a team lead: run their day-to-day coaching, 1:1s, and performance management

 - Drive SQO volume and quality against monthly and quarterly targets, holding the line on our SQO Qualification Guide rather than raw activity counts

 - Run weekly call reviews using our call intelligence platform (Attention), and turn what's working into repeatable technique and kill what isn't

 - Own ramp for new SDR hires in your pod by building the shadowing plan and certify reps in their first 30 days

 - Partner with AE leadership to keep the SDR-to-AE handoff tight, with fewer stalled or disputed SQOs, faster ramp for new AE pods

 - Build and iterate outbound sequences and messaging with Marketing as new segments and verticals open up

 - Report weekly on pipeline generation, SQO conversion, and rep-level performance to our VP Sales

 - Partner with our SDR Manager to keep coaching standards, tooling, and process consistent across both pods
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 2+ years managing an SDR/BDR team in B2B SaaS, ideally at a high-growth startup

 - A track record of hitting or exceeding SQO/pipeline targets with a team of 5+ reps

 - A hands-on coaching style: you're in call recordings and live coaching sessions every week, not just watching a dashboard

 - Fluency with a modern sales stack: CRM (HubSpot or similar), call intelligence (Gong, Attention, or similar), sequencing tools

 - Comfort setting and holding a qualification bar even when it creates short-term friction with reps or AEs

 - Experience hiring and ramping SDRs from scratch: you know what a strong first 30 days looks like

 - Data fluency: comfortable pulling and presenting your own pipeline and conversion numbers without waiting on RevOps

 - Nice to have: home services, contractor, or vertical SaaS background; experience scaling a team through a Series B or similar growth stage
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - You want to build, not maintain: this is a new pod inside a scaling SDR org, not an inherited machine

 - You compete instead of coast: you'll run alongside an established peer pod and want your numbers to hold up next to theirs

 - You give direct, specific feedback and expect the same back

 - You're allergic to sandbagged pipeline and vague activity metrics: you'd rather talk about SQO quality than dial counts

 - You move fast without needing a lot of structure handed to you: you'll build your own cadences and rhythms inside a known framework

 - In-office five days a week in NYC: this is a high-energy, in-person floor and coaching happens live
   
   


BENEFIT

As Avoca grows across NYC and Santa Barbara, the pace and volume of office operations are growing with us. We're hiring a Office Admin to help keep that growth organized, reliable, and employee-friendly — making the day-to-day feel seamless while supporting bigger operational moments like our upcoming office move and internal events.




WHAT YOU'LL DO
